interfaces.name: SudokuEngineAPI
interfaces.description: Core game engine providing puzzle management, move validation, state tracking, and protected cell editing
interfaces.contract: Methods: loadPuzzle(difficulty:string):Puzzle, isFixed(row:int, col:int):boolean, applyMove(row:int, col:int, value:int):MoveResult, getConflicts():CellConflict[], toggleDuplicateHighlight(enabled:boolean):void, resetPuzzle():void, newPuzzle(difficulty:string):Puzzle, getGameState():GameState, incrementMistake():void, getMistakeCount():int, isComplete():boolean. MoveResult = {success:boolean, reason:string} where reason is 'FIXED_CELL' | 'INVALID_RANGE' | 'ALREADY_SET' | 'CONFLICT' | 'OK'

interfaces.name: CellSelectionEvent
interfaces.description: Fired when a user selects a cell

interfaces.name: NumberEntryEvent
interfaces.description: Fired when a user enters a number into a selected cell

interfaces.name: ConflictDetectedEvent
interfaces.description: Fired when duplicate or obvious conflicts are detected in row, column, or box
interfaces.contract: Payload: {conflicts:CellConflict[], highlightEnabled:boolean}. CellConflict = {row:int, col:int, duplicateValue:int, scope:string}

interfaces.name: MistakeIncrementedEvent
interfaces.description: Fired when an invalid move creates a new conflict

interfaces.name: TimerTickEvent
interfaces.description: Fired every second while the timer is running

interfaces.name: PuzzleCompletedEvent
interfaces.description: Fired when the puzzle is solved correctly

interfaces.name: PuzzleDataLibrary
interfaces.description: Static array of curated puzzles embedded in the HTML file
interfaces.contract: Array of objects: {id:string, difficulty:'easy'|'medium'|'hard', board:string[81], solution:string[81]}. Index 0-80 maps to row-major order; '0' represents empty cell.

interfaces.name: LocalStorageDB
interfaces.description: Browser localStorage used to persist last game state and user preferences
interfaces.contract: Key 'sudokuState': JSON serialized GameState. Key 'sudokuPrefs': JSON serialized UserPreferences {duplicateHighlightEnabled:boolean, lastDifficulty:string}. Schema-validated on read; fallback to default state on corruption.

dataContracts.storage: Embedded static JSON array in HTML file
dataContracts.notes: Pre-validated puzzles per difficulty; board/solution encoded as 81-char strings (0=empty); supports offline operation
Properties: value (0-9), isFixed (boolean, true for original givens), isHighlighted (boolean), isConflict (boolean)

dataContracts.storage: In-memory during session; optionally persisted to localStorage on pause/completion
dataContracts.notes: Tracks current board, difficulty, elapsedSeconds, mistakeCount, isPaused, isCompleted, duplicateHighlightEnabled
Stores duplicateHighlightEnabled and lastDifficulty for session continuity
Encapsulates success/failure reason for UI feedback and mistake increment logic
Returned by getConflicts(); used by UI to apply conflict CSS class

scalability.expectedLoad: Single user per browser session; typical interaction <100 moves/minute; timer tick every 1s; load target <1s for HTML file under 200KB
bottlenecks: Initial HTML file parse and script execution on low-end mobile devices
DOM reflow/repaint when updating multiple cells simultaneously (e.g., row/col/box highlight)
localStorage read/write latency on first load
Timer interval drift or throttling when tab is backgrounded
mitigations: Target HTML file size <100KB; minify CSS and JS; inline critical CSS
Use CSS classes for highlighting (batch class toggling) instead of individual style property writes
Defer localStorage read until first user interaction or pause event
Use requestAnimationFrame for timer display updates aligned with browser repaint cycle
Test on WebKit and Gecko at 320px, 768px, and 1440px viewports

securityBaseline.authMethod: None (offline single-file app; no authentication required)
securityBaseline.dataClassification: Public (no PII or sensitive data stored)
threatVectors: Cross-site scripting via malicious puzzle data if injected at build time (NFR-06)
Denial-of-service via pathologically deep validation (not applicable given curated puzzles)
mitigations: Content-Security-Policy meta tag: default-src 'self'; script-src 'unsafe-inline' (required for single-file); style-src 'unsafe-inline'
Strict mode; no eval or Function constructor; validate puzzle array indices (0-80) before access
Validate stored JSON with schema check before use; fallback to default state on corruption
All user inputs (cell values) constrained to 0-9 range before processing

adrs.title: Single-file packaging
adrs.context: Addresses NFR-01 (single HTML file) and NFR-02 (fully offline). FR-02 requires puzzle data without network calls.
adrs.decision: Embed CSS and JavaScript inline; embed puzzle data as a static JSON array within a script tag; use only inline SVG or data URIs for any visual assets; no external CDN or network requests.
adrs.consequences: Larger file size; harder to version individual assets; must validate that total size meets NFR-04 load SLA. Mitigation: minify, inline critical path, lazy-load non-essential UI on first interaction.

adrs.title: Curated puzzle library
adrs.context: FR-02 (three difficulty levels) and non-goals explicitly exclude a sophisticated puzzle generator.
adrs.decision: Package 10+ pre-validated puzzles per difficulty (30 total) as a static array in the HTML. Use a random index selection on 'new puzzle' to ensure variety without runtime generation.
adrs.consequences: Finite puzzle set; users may encounter repeats. Acceptable for MVP scope per contract non-goals.

adrs.title: Cell-fixed protection via engine guard
adrs.context: FR-03 (prevent editing fixed cells). Addresses TPM concern #1.
adrs.decision: SudokuEngineAPI.isFixed(row, col) returns true for original given numbers. SudokuEngineAPI.applyMove() checks isFixed() before mutation. Return MoveResult with success:false and reason:'FIXED_CELL' if cell is fixed. UI ignores input for fixed cells and displays error feedback.
adrs.consequences: Centralizes protection logic; engine is the single source of truth for cell mutability. UI cannot bypass via DOM manipulation.

adrs.title: Conflict/duplicate detection and visual highlighting
adrs.context: FR-06 (optional duplicate highlighting) and FR-09 (mistake counter on invalid moves). Addresses TPM concern #2.
adrs.decision: SudokuEngineAPI.getConflicts() scans current board for duplicate values in rows, columns, and boxes. Returns CellConflict[]. SudokuEngineAPI.applyMove() increments mistake count and fires MistakeIncrementedEvent when a move creates a new conflict. ConflictDetectedEvent propagates conflicts to UI. UI applies CSS class 'conflict' to conflicting cells. Feature is toggleable via toggleDuplicateHighlight(enabled).
adrs.consequences: O(n) scan on each move is acceptable for 81 cells. Toggleable via duplicateHighlightEnabled flag. Mistake counter persists in GameState.

adrs.title: Reset and new-puzzle flows
adrs.context: FR-07 (reset preserves difficulty, new puzzle preserves difficulty) and AC-10. Addresses TPM concern #3.
adrs.decision: SudokuEngineAPI.resetPuzzle() restores board to initial state from stored Puzzle.initialBoard, resets timer to 0, resets mistake count to 0, and fires PuzzleResetEvent. SudokuEngineAPI.newPuzzle(difficulty) selects a new puzzle of the given difficulty (per ADR-002), resets all state, and fires PuzzleLoadedEvent. Both flows preserve the currently selected difficulty level in GameState.
adrs.consequences: Clean separation of concerns; easy to unit test both flows independently. Timer and mistake state encapsulated in GameState.

adrs.title: Explicit mistake tracking interface
adrs.context: FR-09 (mistake counter increments on invalid moves). Addresses TPM concern #4.
adrs.decision: SudokuEngineAPI.incrementMistake() increments internal mistake counter and fires MistakeIncrementedEvent with updated count. getMistakeCount() returns current count. Mistake count resets on resetPuzzle() and newPuzzle(). UI displays mistake count in the game header and includes it in the completion screen payload (via PuzzleCompletedEvent).
adrs.consequences: Mistake count is first-class state alongside timer. Increment logic is centralized in engine and triggered by applyMove() conflict detection.

adrs.title: Performance SLA: load under 1 second
adrs.context: NFR-04 (load instantly under 1 second). Addresses TPM concern #5.
adrs.decision: Target: HTML file <100KB, first contentful paint <500ms, interactive <1s. Achieved via: (1) inline critical CSS, (2) defer non-critical JS execution, (3) minify all code, (4) limit puzzle library to 30 puzzles with compact 81-char string encoding, (5) no localStorage read on initial load. Performance validated via manual timing tests on target browsers.
adrs.consequences: Developer must validate file size on each commit. No automated CI performance gate for MVP; manual testing required.

gaps: Exact puzzle count per difficulty is not defined (ADR-002 assumes 10+ per difficulty)
No automated CI/CD performance budget gate specified (ADR-007 defers to manual testing)
